title: China’s Rongsheng Weighs Alberta-to-BC Pipeline Oil Supply Pact
summary: Rongsheng Petrochemical Co., already the biggest buyer of crude from Canada’s sole West Coast pipeline, is weighing an agreement to buy oil from Alberta’s next project, a proposed million-barrel-a-day pipeline to British Columbia, according to people familiar with the matter.

title: SpaceX IPO Is Said to Draw More Orders Than Shares Available
summary: SpaceX has already received orders for more than the shares available in its $75 billion initial public offering, according to people familiar with the matter, putting the company on the verge of setting the record for the biggest-ever listing.

summary: Mistrust of authorities, funding cuts complicate the response to the virus’s surge.

title: Italy’s Salvini Renews Call for Payments From Intesa, Unicredit
summary: Italy’s Deputy Prime Minister Matteo Salvini said lenders Intesa Sanpaolo SpA and UniCredit SpA should contribute to help boost Italy’s economy.

title: Jobs Report Fuels Fed-Hike Bets, Stocks and Bonds Fall, More
summary: Source: Bloomberg, 0:00

title: Liz Ann Sonders Shares Her ‘Jigsaw Puzzle’ Personal Investment Philosophy
summary: Liz Ann Sonders, chief investment strategist at Charles Schwab, explains how her love of jigsaw puzzles plays into her personal approach to investing. She joins Tom Keene and Scarlet Fu on the debut episode of “Bloomberg Money.” (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Gold Slumps Most in Two Months as Jobs Fuel Fed Rate-Hike Bets
summary: Gold fell the most in more than two months as robust US jobs data fueled bets that the Federal Reserve will likely raise interest rates this year, a headwind for the precious metal.

title: Nasdaq 100 Sinks 3% as Fed-Hike Wagers Lift Yields: Markets Wrap
summary: Wall Street’s historic weekly run came to a halt, with stocks hit by a tech selloff and higher bond yields as a solid jobs report added to bets the Federal Reserve’s next rate move will be a hike.

title: Schwab's Sonders on the Rise of Casino Culture in Markets
summary: Liz Ann Sonders, Charles Schwab chief investment strategist, sees a rise of speculative investing and says financial markets are starting to look more like casinos. She speaks with Tom Keene and Scarlet Fu on the debut episode of "Bloomberg Money." (Source: Bloomberg)

title: TPG, Oaktree, Oak Hill Lead €1 Billion Debt for Evoke Buyout
summary: Private lenders including TPG Credit, Oaktree Capital Management and Oak Hill Advisors are set to provide around €1 billion ($1.2 billion) of funding for the acquisition of gambling firm Evoke Plc by Bally’s Intralot.

title: Nasdaq 100 Falls 3% as Rotation Out of Tech Stocks Continues
summary: The S&P 500’s longest weekly winning streak in four decades was stopped on Friday by the same cohort that’s powered the index’s yearlong advance — technology megacaps.

title: Bloomberg Surveillance 6/5/2026
summary: Jonathan Ferro, Lisa Abramowicz and Annmarie Hordern speak daily with leaders and decision makers from Wall Street to Washington and beyond. No other program better positions investors and executives for the trading day. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: AstraZeneca Broadens US Growth Push
summary: AstraZeneca CFO Aradhana Sarin discusses how the pharmaceutical company is broadening its US shareholder base and plans to generate $80 Billion in revenue by 2030. She speaks with Bloomberg's Katie Greifeld. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Chris Davis on the Importance of Trust
summary:
Barry sits down with Chris Davis,  Chairman and Portfolio Manager at Davis Funds. They discuss his approach to managing risk and the key elements changing the economy. Chris and Barry also discuss Chris's mentors including Charlie Munger, and how he settled into the family business. 
 (Source: Bloomberg)

title: US Prosecutors are Scrutinizing Marks in Private Credit, Clayton Says
summary: Wall Street’s top prosecutor, US Attorney Jay Clayton, says his office is looking at possible valuation discrepancies in the private credit market. The former Chairman of the US Securities and Exchange Commission speaks with Bloomberg News’ Sridhar Natarajan at the Bloomberg Global Credit Forum in New York on June 3. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Ebola Pioneer on Why It’s Not Like Covid
summary:
"Ebola is a deadly virus — but it's not a very contagious virus."

Pioneering Ebola scientist Peter Piot tells Mishal Husain the latest outbreak is different from the Covid pandemic. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Dollar Rises as Jobs Data Boosts Fed Rate-Hike Expectations
summary: The dollar advanced after US jobs data came in stronger than anticipated, signaling further signs of labor market resiliency, and progress in US-Iran peace deal talks stalled.

title: Hormuz Traffic Remains Near Zero as Peace Talks Stall
summary: Commercial traffic through the Strait of Hormuz was limited to just a few ships over the past 24 hours as peace talks between the US and Iran show little sign of advancing.

title: Stocks and Bonds Fall as Jobs Fuel Fed-Hike Bets
summary:
Wall Street’s historic weekly run is poised to come to a halt, with stocks and bonds falling after a solid jobs report added to speculation the Federal Reserve’s next interest-rate move will be a hike.
Kevin Gordon, Head of Macro Research and Strategy for the Schwab Center for Financial Research at Charles Schwab, discusses the equity reaction to May jobs. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: US Hiring Surged in May, Boosting Bets on Fed Rate Hike
summary:
US job growth topped all forecasts in May and the unemployment rate held steady at 4.3%, offering the clearest sign yet that the labor market may be breaking out of a prolonged period of lackluster hiring.
Frances Donald, Chief Economist at RBC, discusses the jobs data and what it signals for the US economy as inflation begins to outpace wage growth. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Morgan Stanley Sees AI-Related Funding Expanding to 15% of All Credit Deals
summary: Scott Goodwin, co-founder and managing partner at Diameter Capital Partners, says that business development companies will face pressure to sell their highest-quality loans to software companies at a discount as banks tighten their grip on the private credit funds. Goodwin speaks with Bloomberg TV's Dani Burger at the Bloomberg Global Credit Forum in New York on June 3. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: GoldenTree's Tananbaum Spots a Credit-Equity Disconnect
summary:
Credit market veteran Steven Tananbaum, founder of GoldenTree Asset Management says that opportunities are emerging in sectors facing fundamental business-model challenges, including software and cable, with some of the most compelling situations arising from mismatches between debt and equity valuations. He spoke with Bloomberg TV's Lisa Abramowicz at the Bloomberg Global Credit Forum in New York on June 3.

 (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Credit Titans Warn of Shakeout on Debt Deals That ‘Don’t Make Sense’
summary: Davidson Kempner's Suzanne Gibbons, Glendon Capital's Holly Kim, Sculptor Capital's Brett Klein and Matthew Rahmani of Perella Weinberg speak with Bloomberg News' Irene Garcia Perez on how credit markets are navigating heightened volatility, higher-for-longer interest rates and the credit market's ``cockroach'' problem at Bloomberg's Global Credit Forum in New York on June 3. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: TAG Immobilien Is Said to Eye Polish IPO Kickoff in Coming Weeks
summary: German real estate group TAG Immobilien AG is considering starting the formal process for a potential initial public offering of its Polish unit Robyg SA in Warsaw as soon as the coming weeks, according to people familiar with the matter.

title: EU Calls on Spain to Cut Gas Use for Stability Post-Blackout
summary: The European Commission has called on Spain to move away from using gas-fired power plants to stabilize its electricity system after last year’s blackout, saying better grids, interconnections and more storage should replace the emergency measures.

title: `There Is No AI Bubble,' Says BI's Rob Schiffman
summary: Bloomberg Intelligence tech credit analyst Robert Schiffman says that the borrowing spree to fund the AI buildout is coming from ``the Mount Rushmore'' of credit and that ``there is no AI bubble.'' He spoke with Bloomberg News' Paula Seligson in a panel alongside BI colleagues Noel Hebert and Jody Lurie at the Bloomberg Global Credit Forum in New York on June 3. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Senegal Makes Foreign-Currency Bond Payments Before IMF Visit
summary: Senegal made payments to bondholders ahead of schedule on two of its foreign-currency notes as the West African nation prepares to host the International Monetary Fund for talks on a new program later this month, according to people familiar with the matter.

title: US Bonds Slide as Hot Jobs Data Fuels Bets on 2026 Fed Hike
summary: Bond traders fully priced in a Federal Reserve interest-rate hike by the end of this year after US job growth topped all forecasts in May, spurring yields higher in the $31 trillion Treasuries market.

title: Fannie, Freddie Whipsaw as Trump Floats Trillion-Dollar Value
summary: Fannie Mae and Freddie Mac common shares rose sharply at the open on Friday after President Donald Trump said the two mortgage finance giants were probably worth $1 trillion, though they subsequently erased much of those gains as skepticism mounted.

title: Nuclear Watchdog Eyes Stronger Reactor Defenses After UAE Attack
summary: Iran’s attack last month on a nuclear power plant in the United Arab Emirates has prompted the world’s atomic watchdog to look at whether reactors’ external power supply lines need additional protection against the threat of war.

title: Oil Falls as Traders Weigh Demand Destruction, Stalled Talks
summary: Oil edged lower as traders assessed the extent of demand destruction against a backdrop of ongoing US-Iran peace talks, which have so far yielded little progress toward reopening the critical Strait of Hormuz.

title: Perú elige entre Fujimori y Sánchez en una votación decisiva
summary: Perú elige presidente este domingo en una votación marcada por la polarización. Keiko Fujimori y Roberto Sánchez representan dos visiones opuestas sobre la economía, la seguridad y el futuro del país. ¿Qué está en juego? Te lo explicamos. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Veja os Assuntos que Marcaram a Semana no Brasil
summary: Confira os assuntos que foram destaque da semana no Brasil com Patricia Xavier. Produção de Barbara Nascimento e Raphael Almeida.  (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Rieder Likens US Economy to 'Three-Month-Old Birthday Cake'
summary: BlackRock Global Fixed Income CIO Rick Rieder says the US economy is tricky right now, comparing it to a three-month-old birthday cake where the icing tastes great but underneath isn't so good. Speaking with Matt Miller on "Bloomberg Open Interest," Rieder says AI will change the ecosystem and the Federal Reserve should pull back a bit to evaluate. (Source: Bloomberg)
